text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. During a code review, developers examine each other's code before it is merged into the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are easier and cheaper to fix. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Less experienced developers learn new techniques from senior colleagues, while everyone gains a better understanding of different parts of the codebase. As a result, the overall quality of the team's work improves, and the project becomes more reliable. In short, regular code reviews make both the code and the team stronger.
